description: From Aurobindo's Q4FY26 earnings presentation, "TheraNym – Our Foray into Biologics Contract Manufacturing" slide: "Strengthening Collaboration with MSD: TheraNym executed an additional product schedule with Merck Sharp & Dohme Singapore Trading (MSD), expanding the existing CMO relationship first announced in May 2024." and "CMO Manufacturing and Supply: Under the schedule, TheraNym will build, operate and supply the drug substance to MSD, further embedding TheraNym in MSD's global supply chain." TheraNym is Aurobindo's biologics CDMO subsidiary; the new schedule adds a dedicated greenfield Drug Substance facility (Unit 2, 60 kL mammalian cell-culture capacity, USD 150-175 Mn capex).

McKesson's fiscal 2026 (ended March 31, 2026) 10-K states: sales to its largest customer, CVS Health Corporation, accounted for approximately 24% of total consolidated revenues, and 21% of total trade receivables, in fiscal 2026. The filing also notes McKesson extended its pharmaceutical distribution partnership with CVS to June 2027 (extension made in fiscal 2023). CVS was McKesson's single largest named customer of the ten largest customers, which together accounted for approximately 73% of total consolidated revenues.

Cardinal Health's fiscal 2025 (ended June 30, 2025) 10-K states: 'Our largest customer, CVS Health, accounted for 30 percent of our fiscal 2025 revenue,' and 26% of gross trade receivables at June 30, 2025. The filing identifies CVS Health Corporation as the only customer individually accounting for at least 10% of revenue and/or gross trade receivables in fiscal 2025, serviced through the Pharmaceutical and Specialty Solutions segment.

Cardinal Health's fiscal 2025 10-K discloses that Cardinal Health and CVS Health Corporation each hold a 50% ownership interest in Red Oak Sourcing, LLC ('Red Oak Sourcing'), a U.S.-based generic-pharmaceutical sourcing venture established in 2014 that negotiates generic pharmaceutical supply contracts on behalf of both companies; the arrangement was amended in August 2021 to extend the term through June 2029.
McKesson's fiscal 2026 10-K, in its Business/Competition section, states: 'Our primary competitors in distribution, wholesaling, and logistics are Cencora, Inc. and Cardinal Health, Inc.'
McKesson's fiscal 2026 10-K, in its Business/Competition section, states: 'Our primary competitors in distribution, wholesaling, and logistics are Cencora, Inc. and Cardinal Health, Inc.'

Cencora's fiscal 2025 (ended September 30, 2025) 10-K, in its Competition section, states: 'Our largest competitors are McKesson Corporation ("McKesson"), Cardinal Health, Inc. ("Cardinal"), and UPS Logistics, among others,' describing the competitive landscape for pharmaceutical distribution.

Cencora's fiscal 2025 10-K, in its Competition section, states: 'Our largest competitors are McKesson Corporation ("McKesson"), Cardinal Health, Inc. ("Cardinal"), and UPS Logistics, among others,' describing the competitive landscape for pharmaceutical distribution.
Cardinal Health's fiscal 2025 10-K states it competes 'with wholesale distributors with national reach, including McKesson Corporation and Cencora, Inc.' in its Pharma segment.
Cardinal Health's fiscal 2025 10-K states it competes 'with wholesale distributors with national reach, including McKesson Corporation and Cencora, Inc.' in its Pharma segment.

Cencora's FY2025 10-K (year ended 2025-09-30) states: "In fiscal 2025, Walgreens and Boots together accounted for approximately 25% of revenue and Evernorth Health Services accounted for approximately 13% of revenue." Cencora's filing never writes "Cigna" or "Express Scripts". The identity binding comes from Cigna's own FY2025 10-K, which states in prose "We have two segments: Evernorth Health Services and Cigna Healthcare" and tags the segment ci:EvernorthMember in its own XBRL. So this edge rests on TWO documents: Cencora's for the dependency and its size, Cigna's for the fact that Evernorth is Cigna.

"The following summarizes revenue, as a percentage of Total revenues, from our three largest U.S. wholesaler customers, which was concentrated in our Biopharma operating segment: ... McKesson, Inc. 25% [2025] 23% [2024] 16% [2023]"
Same table: "Cencora, Inc. 16% [2025] 17% [2024] 12% [2023]" of Pfizer's Total revenues, from Pfizer's three largest U.S. wholesaler customers disclosure.
Same table: "Cardinal Health, Inc. 13% [2025] 14% [2024] 10% [2023]" of Pfizer's Total revenues, from Pfizer's three largest U.S. wholesaler customers disclosure.

"The Company's customers with the largest accounts receivable balances are: McKesson Corporation, Cencora, Inc. and Cardinal Health, Inc., which represented approximately 22%, 21% and 13%, respectively, of total accounts receivable at December 31, 2025." (McKesson = 22%)
Same sentence: Cencora, Inc. represented approximately 21% of Merck's total accounts receivable at December 31, 2025.
Same sentence: Cardinal Health, Inc. represented approximately 13% of Merck's total accounts receivable at December 31, 2025.

"In 2025, 2024, and 2023, three wholesale distributors in the U.S.—McKesson Corporation, Cencora, Inc., and Cardinal Health, Inc.—each accounted for a significant percentage of our consolidated revenue. No other customer accounted for more than 10 percent of our consolidated revenue in any of these years."
Same sentence: Cencora, Inc. is one of Eli Lilly's three named U.S. wholesale distributors each accounting for a significant percentage of consolidated revenue in 2025.
Same sentence: Cardinal Health, Inc. is one of Eli Lilly's three named U.S. wholesale distributors each accounting for a significant percentage of consolidated revenue in 2025.

"In 2025, three wholesale distributors (McKesson Corporation, Cardinal Health, Inc. and Cencora, Inc.) accounted for substantially all of AbbVie's pharmaceutical product sales in the United States. No individual wholesaler accounted for greater than 43% of AbbVie's 2025 gross revenues in the United States."

Same sentence: Cardinal Health, Inc. is one of AbbVie's three named U.S. wholesale distributors accounting for substantially all U.S. pharmaceutical product sales in 2025 (no individual wholesaler over 43%).
Same sentence: Cencora, Inc. is one of AbbVie's three named U.S. wholesale distributors accounting for substantially all U.S. pharmaceutical product sales in 2025 (no individual wholesaler over 43%).
"The following table represents the percentage of consolidated third party net sales to Teva's major customers ... McKesson Corporation 13% [2025] 12% [2024] 9% [2023]"
Same table: "AmerisourceBergen Corporation 11% [2025] 9% [2024] 9% [2023]" of Teva's consolidated third party net sales.

"In total three US national wholesale distributors represent approximately a third of the Group's sales in 2025 and in 2024. The three US national wholesale distributors are McKesson Corp. with CHF 9 billion (2024: CHF 9 billion), Cencora, Inc. with CHF 7 billion (2024: CHF 7 billion) and Cardinal Health, Inc. with CHF 4 billion (2024: CHF 4 billion)."
Same disclosure: Cencora, Inc. sold CHF 7 billion of Roche product in 2025 (CHF 7bn in 2024), one of Roche's three US national wholesale distributors.
Same disclosure: Cardinal Health, Inc. sold CHF 4 billion of Roche product in 2025 (CHF 4bn in 2024), one of Roche's three US national wholesale distributors.

"Selected key customers include Bayer, Bristol-Myers Squibb, GlaxoSmithKline, Haleon, Novo Nordisk, Moderna, Pfizer, and Sarepta Therapeutics." (Catalent FY2024 10-K, describing its CDMO customer base)

"Selected key customers include Bayer, Bristol-Myers Squibb, GlaxoSmithKline, Haleon, Novo Nordisk, Moderna, Pfizer, and Sarepta Therapeutics." Also: "Representative customers of our Biologics segment include Halozyme, Moderna, Novo Nordisk, Regeneron, and Sarepta Therapeutics." Novo Holdings' pending acquisition of Catalent carved out three of Catalent's fill-finish sites (Anagni, Italy; Bloomington, Indiana; Brussels, Belgium) for direct sale to Novo Nordisk A/S.

"In March 2025, Merck acquired the Dundalk, Ireland facility of WuXi Vaccines (a wholly owned subsidiary of WuXi Biologics), which was accounted for as an asset acquisition. Merck paid $437 million at closing which, combined with previous consideration transferred under a prior manufacturing arrangement with WuXi Vaccines related to this facility, resulted in $759 million being recorded as assets under construction..."

"On February 27, 2017, Sanofi and Lonza announced a strategic partnership in the form of a joint venture (BioAtrium AG) to build and operate a large-scale mammalian cell culture facility for monoclonal antibody production in Visp, Switzerland. An initial investment of approximately €0.3 billion ... split 50/50 between the two partners, has now been made in full. In addition, Sanofi could pay BioAtrium AG in the region of €0.8 billion over the 2026-2031 period as its share of operating expenses and the cost of producing future batches." (still in force per Sanofi's FY2025 20-F)

"On 20 March 2024 the Group announced a definitive agreement with Lonza Group AG ('Lonza') under which Lonza would acquire the Genentech manufacturing facility in Vacaville, California, for USD 1.2 billion in conjunction with a manufacturing agreement and related quality services and warehousing. On 1 October 2024 the divestment transaction was completed."

"For 2025, the three largest pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) – Caremark (CVS Health), Express Scripts (Cigna), and OptumRx (United Health Group) – have again each excluded 600 or more drugs from their standard formularies. Formulary exclusions and utilization management are tools used by payers to manage prescription drug costs and leverage their negotiating power with manufacturers." (Sanofi 20-F, Item 4 Competition risk factors)
Same passage: Express Scripts (Cigna) named as one of the three largest US PBMs leveraging formulary-exclusion negotiating power against manufacturers including Sanofi.
Same passage: OptumRx (United Health Group) named as one of the three largest US PBMs leveraging formulary-exclusion negotiating power against manufacturers including Sanofi.

"In April 2024, Rubicon Research Private Limited, Teva Pharmaceuticals, Inc., ... MSN, Aurobindo Pharma Limited, Apitoria Pharma Private Limited and Aurobindo Pharma U.S.A. Inc. (collectively, Aurobindo) and Apotex Inc. and Apotex Corp. ... notified us that they had filed ANDAs with the FDA seeking approval to market generic versions of rimegepant orally disintegrating tablets [Nurtec ODT], claiming noninfringement and/or challenging the validity of some or all of the patents listed."
Same Nurtec (rimegepant) ANDA paragraph: "Teva Pharmaceuticals, Inc." is named among the generic filers notifying Pfizer of ANDAs seeking to market generic rimegepant orally disintegrating tablets.

"[XARELTO ANDA litigation] The following entities are named defendants: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, Inc.; Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, Ltd.; Lupin Limited; ... Teva Pharmaceuticals USA, Inc.; Mylan Pharmaceuticals Inc.; ... Cipla Ltd.; Cipla USA Inc.; ... generic manufacturers who have filed ANDAs seeking approval to market generic versions of XARELTO before expiration of certain Orange Book Listed Patents."
Same XARELTO ANDA litigation paragraph: "Cipla Ltd.; Cipla USA Inc." named as defendants who filed ANDAs seeking to market generic XARELTO before patent expiration.
Same XARELTO ANDA litigation paragraph: "Teva Pharmaceuticals USA, Inc." named as a defendant who filed an ANDA seeking to market generic XARELTO before patent expiration.

"AbbVie Inc. is seeking to enforce patent rights related to ubrogepant (a drug sold under the trademark Ubrelvy). Litigation was filed in the United States District Court for the District of New Jersey in March 2024 against Aurobindo Pharma U.S.A., Inc., Aurobindo Pharma Limited, and Apitoria Pharma Private Limited; ... AbbVie alleges defendants' proposed generic ubrogepant products infringe [certain patents]."

"[Qulipta/atogepant patent litigation] Litigation was filed in the United States District Court for the District of New Jersey in December 2025 and January 2026 against Apotex Inc.; Macleods Pharmaceuticals Ltd. and Macleods Pharma USA, Inc.; and Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, Ltd. and Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, Inc. AbbVie alleges defendants' proposed generic atogepant products infringe certain patents."

"AstraZeneca received Paragraph IV notices relating to patents listed in the FDA Orange Book with reference to Calquence tablets from Cipla USA, Inc. and Cipla Limited (collectively, Cipla) in April 2024 ... AstraZeneca filed patent infringement lawsuits against Cipla in May 2024 ... AstraZeneca alleges that a generic version of Calquence tablets, if approved and marketed, would infringe patents."

"[Forxiga patent proceedings, US] In December 2023, AstraZeneca initiated ANDA litigation against Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. and Sun Pharmaceutical Industries, Inc. in the District Court. No trial date has been set."

"We received notice letters from two ANDA filers regarding the filing of their ANDAs with paragraph (IV) certifications for certain of the patents listed in the Orange Book for AUSTEDO. In 2022, we reached agreements with Lupin and Aurobindo, respectively, to sell their generic products beginning in April 2033, or earlier under certain circumstances."

"Teva also settled litigation against three 505(b)(2) applicants: Hospira, Inc. ('Hospira'), Dr. Reddy's Laboratories ('DRL') and Accord Healthcare ('Accord'). Based on these settlement agreements, Hospira, Accord and DRL can launch their products on November 17, 2027, or earlier under certain circumstances."
